title: vernonia patula
intro: Vernonia patula, also known as the Rough Ironweed, is a perennial herb native to the southeastern United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Vernonia patula include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system, making it useful for treating conditions such as arthritis and wound healing. Vernonia patula is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and it can also be used topically as a salve or poultice to aid in wound healing and skin issues.
